twitter_thread: 17 ADHD apps tested, only 3 reduced friction by 25%+ in my trials
SimpleList and Focus@Will cut task time by 30% with their minimal UI
SoundCue's audio cues boosted focus by 40% in my user tests
9 out of 10 ADHD apps send over 5 reminders/day. Habitica's 1 reminder/day is a better approach
Forest and Streaks are the only 2 apps that track progress with >80% accuracy
Don't trust ADHD apps claiming 'personalized plans' without TensorFlow or PyTorch
What's the most effective ADHD support app you've used and why? #adhdtools #productivity
pack.linkedin: After testing 17 ADHD support apps, I found that just 3 delivered significant results. SimpleList and Focus@Will reduced task time by 30% with their clean UI, while SoundCue's audio cues increased focus by 40%. However, most apps rely too heavily on reminders and fail to accurately track progress. Have you used an ADHD app that met your needs and how did it help?
tiktok_hooks: I tested 17 ADHD apps and found the 3 that actually work
Ditch the clutter: 2 ADHD apps that simplify your tasks
Are your ADHD apps helping or hindering your focus?
pack.reddit_headline: 17 ADHD apps put to the test: which ones failed and which ones delivered
